description: v1.5.0 perf baseline (P5-T9). Captured against the 1k stress fixture (1000 ConfigMaps + 100 Deployments, replicas=0, in the petclinic-stress namespace) on the Docker Desktop Kubernetes cluster (k8s v1.36.1) under WSL2. Tier 1 is the memory-backed v1.5 binary run locally against the cluster; Tier 2 is the same binary in postgres mode against a standalone apache/age:release_PG16_1.6.0 Postgres+AGE pod reached over kubectl port-forward (which adds per-round-trip WSL2 networking overhead). This baseline confirms the GraphStore v2 interface rename (P5-T1..T3) introduced no regression: both tiers meet every spec target with large headroom — Tier 2 cluster_view p95 ~29ms, namespace_view p95 ~39ms, blast_radius p95 ~4ms, all far under the 1000ms/500ms SLOs — and /api/v1/info reports graphstore_version="v2" while migration 010 (otel_runtime_edges) and the otel_spans table applied cleanly to the AGE database. The OTel overlay (F-204) is not exercised here: correlation runs as a detached background job (<=1min cadence, off the request path — not a request-latency SLO), and the /api/v1/otel/overlay READ endpoint is a cheap Tier-2 gate (~3ms 503 when otel.enabled=false). NOTE: this is a 1k fixture, deliberately lighter than the v1.4 10k baseline to spare a local dev cluster — the numbers demonstrate SLO compliance and no v2-store regression, not a direct magnitude comparison to the v1.4 10k run; a 10k re-run belongs in CI / the release environment.

spec_targets._comment: The v1.2 reclaimed SLOs, carried forward unchanged: cluster_view / namespace_view p95 <= 1000ms, blast_radius p95 <= 500ms. v1.5 meets them on both tiers with large headroom.
